text: When you write code, you will often find bugs—errors that stop your program from working correctly. The first step is to identify the bug by understanding what the program should do and what it actually does. A systematic approach is helpful: test small parts of your code one at a time to find where the problem starts. Debugging tools, such as breakpoints and variable inspectors, can show you what happens step by step. You can also use logging to print messages at key points in your code. This lets you track values and see where things go wrong. With practice, debugging becomes a logical process, not a guessing game.
